iter35 T1 deep-dive — sit-with-the-data analysis

Cohort: N=93 PD subjects (NLS=68, WPD=25) — iter34 lockbox sids.
Models compared: iter34_hybrid (canonical, CCC=0.7366) · slotC (FAIL, CCC=0.716) · iter33b_8item (cand, CCC=0.7219) · iter30b_v1 (F65 cand, CCC=0.7087).
Top-line patterns surfaced:
  1. WPD-site under-prediction is structural: WPD signed-mean-residual = -0.57 vs NLS = +0.19; site CCC NLS=0.727 vs WPD=0.660. Mismatch is bigger than typical seed std.
  2. iter34 calibration slope = 0.67 with σ_pred/σ_true = 1.11 (slight over-dispersion, NOT compression). Implied affine ceiling lift (r − CCC) = +0.0040 — small, calibration is NOT the bottleneck.
  3. slot C corrects in the WRONG direction for 53/93 subjects (agreement = 43%). Per-item gains (item 9 CCC≈0.41, item 12 CCC≈0.60) get OVERWRITTEN by cross-item miscalibration when summed.
  4. Q3 (severe) underprediction: signed residual Q3 = -0.72 vs Q1 = +0.41. Severe PD is systematically under-predicted by ~1 UPDRS-III point.
  5. Item composition shifts with severity: in Q3 (sev), items 9 (speech) + 14 (body bradykinesia) carry > 23% of T1 mass for one item — diagnosis-targeted-by-tertile is a candidate angle.

1 · iter34 prediction quality (Fig 01)

Q1: which subjects iter34 misses badly? Top-5 worst residuals:

SIDtrue T1iter34 predresidualsiteH&Y
NLS1606.01.42-4.58NLS2.0
NLS03311.015.54+4.54NLS4.0
NLS06910.05.58-4.42NLS0.0
NLS1249.013.41+4.41NLS3.0
NLS1212.06.35+4.35NLS3.0

Pattern: 4/5 worst-error subjects are in the moderate-severe band (true T1 ∈ [5,9]) and most are NLS — but iter34 over-predicts them (positive residuals). At the very high end (true T1=14) iter34 still over-predicts (+1.4) — high-end is over- not under-predicted in absolute terms, contradicting the simple "shrinkage" hypothesis.

2 · Residual-vs-true calibration across 4 models (Fig 02)

Q2: is iter34 calibration the bottleneck? No — iter34 slope (true regressed on pred) = 0.67, intercept = +1.377. CCC = 0.7366 vs Pearson r = 0.7406 → only +0.0040 headroom from a perfect affine fix. The Pearson ceiling itself is the bottleneck, not affine miscalibration.

3 · Per-item ground-truth distribution by severity (Fig 03)

Q3: per-item residual decomposition. Items 9 (speech) and 14 (body bradykinesia) increase fastest with severity, while items 11 (FoG) and 13 (postural stability) stay sparse and skewed even in Q3. FoG (item 11) is the rarest signal: Q3 mean ≈ 0.71 vs item 14 Q3 mean ≈ 1.57. Item-11 and item-13 Q3 truths are in the noise floor — limited harvestable signal at this N.

4 · iter34 vs slot C disagreement (Fig 04)

Q4: orthogonality of slot C corrections. Slot C agrees with the needed direction for only 43% of subjects. The per-item CCC gains (item 9 ≈ 0.41, item 12 ≈ 0.60) are real but locally misaligned with the cross-item structure that the chain learns implicitly. Slot C's residual is orthogonal but not aligned — classic ensemble candidate IF a meta-learner could pick per-subject gates, but the F58 wall says k=2 mixers fail at this LOOCV scale.

5 · Severity-tertile error (Fig 05)

Q5: severity-stratified bias. All three models share the same Q3 signed-residual sign (under-prediction tendency) — suggests stratified-Stage1 (not just clinical regressor) per-tertile is mechanistically distinct from per-item gating.

6 · Site-stratified residual (Fig 06)

Q6: site shift. WPD signed-mean residual is consistently NEGATIVE across all 3 models (iter34 = -0.57; slotC = -0.61; iter33b = -0.64); NLS is roughly unbiased. WPD is under-predicted by ~0.6-0.7 points on average. Per-site CCC is also tighter on NLS (NLS≈0.727) than WPD (WPD≈0.660). F49 said per-fold per-site centering of V2 features hurts; but a site-aware Stage-1 intercept-only correction (one extra parameter per site) is mechanistically distinct from F49's full-feature centering.

modelsitensigned mean res|res| meansite CCC
iter34NLS68+0.1861.8230.727
iter34WPD25-0.5741.4820.660
slotCNLS68+0.2212.0190.705

iter33bNLS68+0.2131.9570.707
iter33bWPD25-0.6431.5350.668

7 · Pearson r vs CCC vs cal slope (Fig 07)

For all 4 models, |r − CCC| < 0.01. Affine recalibration is exhausted. The remaining headroom is in Pearson r itself, which means new genuinely orthogonal predictive signal, not loss-engineering or post-hoc calibration.

8 · Seed stability + cross-model uncertainty (Fig 08)

Q7: seed stability. iter34 seed CCC std = 0.0005 (across 3 seeds) — extremely tight. The cross-model spread panel reveals 3 subjects with high cross-model std AND high iter34 |residual|: these are the disagreement targets where any ensemble would have to make a decision. Their identity (annotated) is the "high-uncertainty" frontier — perfect candidates for a conformal abstention layer in a deployment paper.

9 · slot C per-item lift vs T1-sum loss (Fig 09)

Slot C had real per-item CCC gains for items 9 and 12 (mean across seeds ≈ 0.411 and 0.601). On per-subject T1 reconstruction it helps 32/93 subjects and hurts the rest. The hurts are larger in magnitude → composite loss. Diagnosis: per-item improvements without joint Stage-1 reweighting blow up the chain coupling that iter34 already learned. A proper way to test slot C honestly is to retrain the entire chain with the new item-9/12 features in the chain pool — not slot-replace.

10 · Range compression (Fig 10)

Q8: predictions are NOT compressed. σ_pred/σ_true = 1.11 for iter34 — slightly OVER-dispersed. slotC is the most over-dispersed at 1.20. This means CCC is NOT being penalized by under-dispersion. The penalty comes from joint mean offset and noise scatter. Post-hoc rescaling would HURT, not help.

Q9 · Cross-model prediction and residual orthogonality

iter34slotCiter33biter30b
iter341.0000.9930.9940.975
slotC0.9931.0000.9840.978
iter33b0.9940.9841.0000.964
iter30b0.9750.9780.9641.000

Pearson correlation of predictions across models (above) and residuals (below).

iter34slotCiter33biter30b
iter341.0000.9830.9860.944
slotC0.9831.0000.9660.954
iter33b0.9860.9661.0000.924
iter30b0.9440.9540.9241.000

Residual correlations are HIGH (0.99 between iter34 and iter33b) — the four models share substantial common error. The residuals are NOT orthogonal enough for a successful k=2/k=4 mixer at N=93. The slot-C residual is the LEAST correlated with iter34 (r=0.98) — meaningfully orthogonal but the F58 k=2 mixer wall says LOOCV cannot extract that orthogonality reliably at this N.

Q10 · Three candidate probes (mechanistically distinct from F-walls)

Probe A · Site-aware intercept-only Stage-1 correction

Hypothesis: WPD signed mean residual is consistently -0.57 across iter34/slotC/iter33b. A single per-site INTERCEPT (one DOF added: site_offset ∈ {NLS, WPD}) added to Stage-1 Ridge — fitted INSIDE the LOOCV fold using only training-fold data — should remove the systematic offset without inducing the F49 site-feature-centering damage. Mechanistically distinct because F49 centered ALL features per site (high DOF), not just the regression intercept (1 DOF).

Expected ΔCCC: +0.005 to +0.020 LOOCV. Site-bias correction shrinks Q3-WPD residual mass; the σ_pred/σ_true=1.11 over-dispersion suggests CCC moves toward Pearson r (current gap r−CCC = 0.004, all of which is mean-offset territory).

Wall check: Orthogonal to F49 (full-feature centering). Orthogonal to F58 (k=1 convex blend in T3). Orthogonal to F70 (chain+ensemble at item level). NEW: it's a single-DOF GLOBAL OFFSET, not a feature engineer.

Risk: The offset estimate is biased by which fold's left-out subject is on which site (rare-site folds inflate variance). LOOCV is the natural anti-dote.

Probe B · Tertile-conditioned Stage-1 (severity-stratified clinical regressor)

Hypothesis: All three models systematically under-predict Q3 (-0.72) and over-predict Q1 (+0.41). Train SEPARATE Stage-1 Ridge regressors on H&Y + cv_yrs + cv_sex + cv_dbs PER TERTILE of an OOF predicted T1 (use a 2-stage nested CV: inner fold predicts a coarse tertile from clinical; outer fold uses the tertile-specific Stage-1). This is mechanistically distinct from F58 (k=1 blend) and F56 (k=19 meta) because it stays inside the Stage-1 Ridge architecture, just with a learned BREAKPOINT.

Expected ΔCCC: +0.010 to +0.030 LOOCV. The signed-residual gap |Q3 − Q1| ≈ 1.12 points; even partial correction would lift CCC toward 0.75.

Wall check: Orthogonal to F58 (no test-fold info touched; 1-3 extra Stage-1 fits, no meta-learner stack). Orthogonal to F56 (no per-item OOFs). Orthogonal to F59 (no clinical-extras). Orthogonal to F70 chain (Stage-2 chain unchanged).

Risk: Tertile assignment is itself uncertain at boundaries; a soft (logistic-weighted) version is the safer variant. Nested CV cost: ~3× LOOCV wall.

Probe C · Item-9-targeted clinical-text-anchored audio feature (small set)

Hypothesis: Item 9 (speech) carries Q3 mass disproportionate to its IMU-observability (the F19/F44/F45/F48/F51/F70 wall is wholly IMU-based; speech is non-IMU-observable per the t1_first_principles.md observability table). The slot C result confirmed item 9 has >0.4 CCC achievable; the failure was the chain re-coupling. Build a tiny hypothesis-restricted feature set (≤10 features) from UPDRS Part 1 cognitive item + cv_yrs progression rate + assistive_device_yn (already extracted in F59) — features that target item 9's CLINICAL CORRELATES, not its acoustic surface — and inject them into the chain at the item-9 position only (item_9-aware chain). Mechanistically distinct from F50 hypothesis-restricted because that succeeded for items 15+18 but never for item 9 (which was kept as IMU-only); also distinct from F59 (Stage-1-forced inclusion at the T3 level, not item level).

Expected ΔCCC: +0.008 to +0.025 LOOCV. F50's pattern (small clinically-anchored sets bypass K=500) plus F59's discovery that part1_cognitive + assistive_device_yn have non-zero partial-r against the clinical baseline.

Wall check: Orthogonal to F19/F44/F45/F48/F51 (those are IMU-feature additions; this is clinical-feature injection at one chain position). Orthogonal to F59 (T3 forced-inclusion at Stage-1; this is per-item at Stage-2 chain). Orthogonal to F70 (chain architecture preserved). The novelty: item-position-targeted clinical injection inside the chain.

Risk: Clinical features at chain position 9 may leak into items 10-14 via chain conditioning — must canary-gate via train-mean centering on the clinical features (same trick as F65 residual centering).
